consts:
Compression codec for the HNeRV decoder + per-frame-pair latents.

Decoder path: per-tensor symmetric INT8 quantization → zigzag → concat with
shape/scale metadata → brotli (quality 11). We previously used a hybrid that
added per-tensor categorical AC (via the constriction crate) for big tensors —
that was ~217 bytes smaller (~+0.0001 to score) but added a Python dependency
and embedded a feature-level compression choice into the inflation path. Pure
brotli is the simpler, more transparent default.

Latent path: per-dim min/max scaling to [0, 254] (uint8), then 1st-order
temporal delta, zigzag to uint16, split into lo/hi byte streams (lo brotli's
well, hi is mostly zero). Beats plain brotli by ~240 bytes on our latents.

Round-trip verified bit-exact.
doc:
Compression codec for the HNeRV decoder + per-frame-pair latents.

Decoder path: per-tensor symmetric INT8 quantization → zigzag → concat with
shape/scale metadata → brotli (quality 11). We previously used a hybrid that
added per-tensor categorical AC (via the constriction crate) for big tensors —
that was ~217 bytes smaller (~+0.0001 to score) but added a Python dependency
and embedded a feature-level compression choice into the inflation path. Pure
brotli is the simpler, more transparent default.

Latent path: per-dim min/max scaling to [0, 254] (uint8), then 1st-order
temporal delta, zigzag to uint16, split into lo/hi byte streams (lo brotli's
well, hi is mostly zero). Beats plain brotli by ~240 bytes on our latents.

Round-trip verified bit-exact.

        >>   38 FOR_ITER               225 (to 492)

child_codes.name: _decode_compact_decoder
child_codes.doc:
Decode compact architecture-ordered INT8 weights.

    The tensor order and shapes are defined by HNeRVDecoder, so this format
    stores only one scale per tensor plus the zigzagged INT8 payload.
child_codes.dis:
74           0 RESUME                   0

 80           2 LOAD_CONST               1 (0)
              4 LOAD_CONST               2 (('HNeRVDecoder',))
              6 IMPORT_NAME              0 (model)
              8 IMPORT_FROM              1 (HNeRVDecoder)
             10 STORE_FAST               2 (HNeRVDecoder)
             12 POP_TOP

 82          14 LOAD_GLOBAL              5 (NULL + io)
             24 LOAD_ATTR                6 (BytesIO)
             44 LOAD_FAST                0 (raw)
             46 CALL                     1
             54 STORE_FAST               3 (buf)

             58 LOAD_ATTR                9 (NULL|self + read)
             78 LOAD_CONST               3 (3)
             80 CALL                     1
             88 STORE_FAST               4 (magic)

 84          90 LOAD_FAST                4 (magic)

             98 POP_JUMP_IF_FALSE       11 (to 122)

 85         100 LOAD_GLOBAL             11 (NULL + ValueError)
            110 LOAD_CONST               5 ('bad compact decoder magic')

 87         202 LOAD_GLOBAL             13 (NULL + struct)

            256 LOAD_CONST               9 (4)

 88         282 PUSH_NULL
            284 LOAD_FAST                2 (HNeRVDecoder)

 89         286 LOAD_FAST                1 (meta)

child_codes.name: encode_latents
child_codes.doc:
Encode (n_pairs, latent_dim) float tensor to bytes.

    Per-dim asymmetric UINT8 (min/max scaling to [0,254]) + 1st-order temporal
    delta + zigzag to uint16 + lo/hi byte split (lo brotli's well, hi mostly 0).

module_dis:
0           0 RESUME                   0

  1           2 LOAD_CONST               0 ("Compression codec for the HNeRV decoder + per-frame-pair latents.\n\nDecoder path: per-tensor symmetric INT8 quantization → zigzag → concat with\nshape/scale metadata → brotli (quality 11). We previously used a hybrid that\nadded per-tensor categorical AC (via the constriction crate) for big tensors —\nthat was ~217 bytes smaller (~+0.0001 to score) but added a Python dependency\nand embedded a feature-level compression choice into the inflation path. Pure\nbrotli is the simpler, more transparent default.\n\nLatent path: per-dim min/max scaling to [0, 254] (uint8), then 1st-order\ntemporal delta, zigzag to uint16, split into lo/hi byte streams (lo brotli's\nwell, hi is mostly zero). Beats plain brotli by ~240 bytes on our latents.\n\nRound-trip verified bit-exact.\n")
